title: The Godmother of AI | Princeton Alumni
excerpts:
Section Title: The Godmother of AI > The Godmother of AI
Content:
As the [Sequoia Capital Professor in Computer Science at Stanford University](https://profiles.stanford.edu/fei-fei-li) and co-director of the [Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence](https://hai.stanford.edu/) (HAI) — an interdisciplinary research hub that engages the social sciences and humanities — she’s spent more than 25 years doing just that while driving breakthroughs in cognitively inspired AI, computer vision, deep learning and AI systems for healthcare delivery. She is an elected member of the National Academy of Engineering, the National Academy of Medicine and the American Academy of Arts and Sciences. In 2023, she was the recipient of the Intel Innovation Lifetime Achievement Award.

title: Speaker Details: Google Cloud Next 2026
excerpts:
Section Title: Speaker Details
Content:
Dr. Fei- Fei Li is the godmother of artificial intelligence. As the creator of ImageNet, the database that taught computers to “see,” she laid the groundwork for modern AI marvels such as Google Lens, DALL- E, selfdriving cars, and facial recognition. In her current role as co-founder and CEO of World Labs, she’s building the foundation for AI’s next major breakthrough — Large World Models (LWMs) that can understand and interact with the physical world. A champion for AI that is safe and inclusive, Dr. Li is also the founding co- director of Stanford’s Human- Centered AI Institute (HAI) and the founder of AI4ALL. Delivering depth and breadth on both the technical and human aspects of the technology, Dr. Li leaves audiences awestruck, informed, and empowered to leverage AI in their unique industries.

title: Spatial intelligence, says Fei-Fei Li, is the next frontier in AI - Fast Company
excerpts:
Mar 24, 2026 — AI pioneer Fei-Fei Li and her World Labs team are taking artificial intelligence beyond mere words to achieve spatial intelligence.
"We firmly believe that spatial intelligence is the next frontier of artificial intelligence," says [Fei-Fei Li](https://www.fastcompany.com/user/fei-fei-li) , the AI pioneer whose work laid the foundation for computer vision systems.
AI pioneer Fei-Fei Li and her World Labs team are taking artificial intelligence beyond mere words to achieve spatial intelligence.
SHARE
Li's World Labs, which she cofounded with AI researchers Justin Johnson, Christoph Lassner, and Ben Mildenhall two years ago, is building a form of "world model" capable of processing spatial data and developing a physics-based understanding of three-dimensional environments.
Spatial intelligence is the next frontier of AI, says World Labs' Fei-Fei Li

title: From Words to Worlds: Spatial Intelligence is AI’s Next Frontier
excerpts:
Section Title: From Words to Worlds: Spatial Intelligence is AI’s Next Frontier
Content:
The pursuit of visual and spatial intelligence has been the North Star guiding me since I entered the field. It’s why I spent years building ImageNet, the first large-scale visual learning and benchmarking dataset and one of three key elements enabling the birth of modern AI, along with neural network algorithms and modern compute like graphics processing units (GPUs). It’s why [my academic lab at Stanford](https://svl.stanford.edu/) has spent the last decade combining computer vision with robotic learning. And it’s why my cofounders Justin Johnson, Christoph Lassner, Ben Mildenhall, and I created [World Labs](https://www.worldlabs.ai/) more than one year ago: to realize this possibility in full, for the first time.

title: Fei-Fei Li - Cofounder/CEO, World Labs; AI Researcher ... - LinkedIn
excerpts:
Section Title: Fei-Fei Li > Activity > 115K followers
Content:
this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/wenlongh_what-representation-enables-open-world-robot-activity-7441998081681911808-PWPa)What representation enables open-world robot manipulation from generated videos? Introducing Dream2Flow, our recent work that bridges video generation and robot control with 3D object flow. 🌐 [dream2flow.github.io](http://dream2flow.github.io?trk=public_profile__posts-text) by [Stanford University](https://www.linkedin.com/school/stanford-university/?trk=public_profile__posts-text) 🔹Robot manipulation is about inducing changes in an environment through actions. We observe that video models (e.g., Veo) excel at producing plausible object motions from an in-the-wild image and language instructions. Intriguingly, these motions are more physically realistic when the actor is human rather than robot, likely because the internet contains far more human interaction data than robot data.

***Historic Keynote with Geoffrey Hinton, Fei-Fei Li, and Andrew Ng Headlines Comprehensive Program Focused on Real-World AI Deployment, Governance, and Innovation***
**LAS VEGAS, NV / [ACCESS Newswire](https://www.accessnewswire.com/) / March 23, 2026 /** The conference agenda for [**Ai4 2026**](https://pr.report/jzny) , America's largest AI conference focused on the real-world applications of artificial intelligence, is now live. Taking place August 4-6, 2026, at The Venetian Las Vegas, the [**three-day program**](https://pr.report/jznz) will convene the global leaders shaping how AI is built, deployed, and governed across industries.

title: Fei-Fei Li
excerpts:
Section Title: Early life and education > Career and research > Research
Content:
Li's research in computer vision contributed to a line of work called Natural Scene Understanding, or later, story-telling of images. <sup>[[ 84 ]]()</sup> She was recognized for her work in this area by the International Association for Pattern Recognition in 2016. <sup>[[ 85 ]]()</sup> She delivered a talk on the main stage of TED in Vancouver in 2015, and has since then been viewed more than 2 million times. <sup>[[ 85 ]]()</sup>
In recent years, Fei-Fei Li's research work expanded to artificial intelligence in healthcare , collaborating closely with Stanford University School of Medicine professor Arnold Milstein. <sup>[[ 86 ]]()</sup> She has also worked on improving bias in image recognition, for instance by removing concepts with low imageability from ImageNet. <sup>[[ 87 ]]()</sup>
